meta.title: Guatemala Spanish 3000 Study Pack
meta.description: Mobile-friendly vocabulary tracker: 3,000 high-frequency words, coffee-shop phrases, and Guatemala notes.

nice to meet you / much pleasure

no idea / not even an idea

just like you / same as you

to love; have great affection for

coffeePhrases.spanish: Buenas, ¿me da un café negro, por favor?
coffeePhrases.english: Hi/hello, can I have a black coffee, please?

coffeePhrases.spanish: Quiero un café pequeño, por favor.
coffeePhrases.english: I want a small coffee, please.

coffeePhrases.spanish: Quiero un café mediano, por favor.
coffeePhrases.english: I want a medium coffee, please.

coffeePhrases.spanish: Quiero un café grande, por favor.
coffeePhrases.english: I want a large coffee, please.
I want a coffee with milk.
Can you make that to go?
Do you have any sweet bread left?
What do you recommend for breakfast?
Can I work here for a little while?
Could you bring me water, please?
Could you bring me a spoon, please?
Do you have an extra napkin?

coffeePhrases.spanish: ¿Me avisa cuando esté listo, por favor?
coffeePhrases.english: Please let me know when it is ready.
Can you give me the receipt?
Can you charge us together or separately?
I come here to practice my Spanish.

coffeePhrases.spanish: Hoy quiero practicar solo en español.
coffeePhrases.english: Today I want to practice only in Spanish.
If you want, you can correct me.

coffeePhrases.spanish: ¿Puede hablar un poco más despacio, por favor?
coffeePhrases.english: Could you speak a little more slowly, please?
I didn’t understand. Can you repeat that?
How do you say [word] in Spanish?
Do people say it like that in Guatemala?
Which word do people use more here?
Do you use tú or vos?
A polite Guatemala-style way to ask for a coffee.

conversationVerbs.spanish: Quiero ser más fluido en español.
conversationVerbs.english: I want to be more fluent in Spanish.
I was looking for this café.
How do you say [word] in Spanish?
I want a coffee with milk.
I want to practice with you.
I want to learn useful verbs.
What would you recommend to me?

conversationVerbs.spanish: Voy a la cafetería cada mañana.
conversationVerbs.english: I go to the coffee shop every morning.
I’m going to stay a while.
How do I get to the market?
Are you going to come tomorrow?
I come to practice my Spanish.
I make coffee at home too.
What can I do to improve?

conversationVerbs.spanish: Digo una frase y usted me corrige.
conversationVerbs.english: I say a sentence and you correct me.
I don’t know that word yet.
I already know how to order coffee.
I want to get to know the city better.
Do you know a quiet place to study?

conversationVerbs.spanish: Me gusta practicar en lugares reales.
conversationVerbs.english: I like practicing in real places.
It’s close to me / it’s nearby.
Where do I put the cup?
I feel that I understand more.
That seems like a good idea to me.

guatemalaBonus.note: Used a lot with friends, relatives, and trusted people.
You will hear both tú and vos in Guatemala.
Very useful in shops, with older people, and in polite situations.
Examples of voseo-style verb forms you will hear in Guatemala.
Friendly identity word for someone from Guatemala.
Common everyday word for a young person.
No tengo pisto. = I don’t have cash.
Casual way to tell someone to hush.
In Guatemala it can mean “please give/sell me …” when ordering.
